verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin is a secreted dimeric glycoprotein, but the NCBI summary explicitly notes a dimeric/multimeric form at the cell surface in addition to plasma and ECM. FN1 binds cell-surface integrins (α5β1 and others) and assembles into fibrils on the cell surface via integrin-driven fibrillogenesis, a wash-resistant, non-equilibrium process producing insoluble surface-attached fibronectin matrix. The EDB+ oncofetal isoform is exploited clinically (radioligand/ADC programs, e.g., L19-based agents) by binding cell-/tumor-surface-deposited FN. This is stable post-secretion attachment to surface-anchored partners (integrins) and pericellular matrix, satisfying stable_surface_attachment rather than secreted_only. Not a TM protein itself; surface presence is context- and partner-dependent.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Borderline between stable_surface_attachment (integrin-anchored fibrillogenesis on cell surface) and secreted_only (bulk in plasma/ECM).
